problem: `/mc prove_trade_ready` and `/mc why_no_candidates` outputs can drift as fields evolve, risking Telegram contract breakage and operator confusion.

evidence.symptom: Recent rapid evolution introduced key renames/additions; no schema-lock tests currently enforce compatibility.

required_change: Define explicit JSON schemas (or golden contract assertions) for `mc_command --json` prove-trade-ready fields and `mc_why` diagnostic fields.
Add tests that fail on missing/renamed required keys: trace_ids, spot_integrity, mc_provenance mandatory fields, trade_ready_rule core metrics, no-candidates reason path.
Add backward-compat mapping test for legacy keys where needed.
Document contract in a reference file under `docs/` or `kb/` and link in test comments.
acceptance_tests: Schema tests pass and fail intentionally when required keys are removed locally.
Manual run: `python3 scripts/mc_command.py --max-attempts 1 --retry-delay-sec 1 --json` includes all required keys.
Manual run: `python3 scripts/mc_why.py` prints schema-locked diagnostics including stale-source marker.
constraints: Do not change Telegram-facing key names without compatibility tests.
No gate-threshold changes in this ticket.
Fail closed on schema mismatch in tests.
